EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface trough lingering near the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lisa from the Emergency Operations Center reported flooding conditions at Cost-u-less and a minor mudslide at the governors mansion.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Mid to upper level low pressure centered just southeast of Tutuila island, causing instability.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Calls received of flooded roads near the airport and Cost U Less due to heavy rainfall. WSO Pago Pago recorded 1.81 of rainfall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Choppy seas from the strong trade winds caused seas to reach over 15 feet with periods  around 11 seconds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reports in Coconut Point, Nuuuli of coastal inundation in front yards and graves completely underwater.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm near New Zealand produced swell periods of 14 to 16 seconds, which generated high surfs around 14 ft.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: News from the talanei reported two boys swept out to sea during high surf conditions due to strong rip currents.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure to the south interacts with a surface trough to the north causing high winds over the territory.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Plane went into airport pond due to high winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure to the south interacts with a surface trough to the north causing high winds over the territory.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: USCG reported increased frequency of winds in the Pago Pago Harbor. Wise Honest Vessel anchored in the harbor was swaying back and forth due to strong winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An active surface trough remains over the islands through tonight.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: WSO Pago Pago recorded 2.16 inches of rain within the hour. Teachers reported flooding at Tafuna High School and outside of classrooms. Midkiff elementary reports flooding near classrooms. Fagaima resident reported flooding in the front of her home about 1 foot deep. Hard for cars to drive through.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An active surface trough ushered heavy showers over the territory.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: EOC called with 2 mudslides ; first in ASAO, the second at Alataua elementary at Nua and Seetaga. Mudslide reported damage to Alataua elementary library.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A trough associated with a storm system near New Zealand ushered heavy rain over the territory.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Weather Service Office Pago Pago recorded 2.20 inches of rain within in the 18 hour period.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms embedded within an active trough shy east-northeast of Tutuila produced flash flooding conditions over the territory. A strong high pressure system southeast of the territory held this feature over the island chain during this period.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The national weather service recorded 3.12 inches of rain at the Tafuna Station within the 24 hour period. All residence along flood prone areas were encouraged to evacuate to higher grounds as low lying areas around the island experienced flash flooding conditions. Flooded roadways were also reported by numerous callers and NWS employees making their way over to the office.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An active trough over the island territory ushered heavy showers with the bulk of the rain dumped on Manu'a islands. However, as Manu'a has no official rain gauge reports, we went off of the reports from the usual skywarn spotters.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Although 1.53 inches of rain was recorded at the weather service office located on the island of Tutuila, satellite imagery showed strong convective clouds and cold cloud top temperatures indicating most of the rain being dumped over Manu'a. One of our trained spotters called to report that the front of her house was flooded and low cars could not go passed her house to get to the other side of the roadway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm near New Zealand produced swell periods of 14 to 16 seconds, which generated high surfs around 14 ft.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A canoe with 6 rowers capsized in Utulei due to rough seas. Rowers had a difficult time flipping canoe back to its upright position.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Surfs up to 18 feet impacted coastal shores due to large southerly swells.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Meteorologist Hans reported high surf impacting roadways. Debris from surfs made it difficult for drivers.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge south of the territory ushered strong southeasterly trades of 25 to 35 kts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: MIC Elinor reported downed banana trees in Utulei due to strong winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep convection and rain west of the Samoan islands ushered heavy showers across the territory.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous callers reported flooded roadways. One caller from the village of Leone reported flooding in his home due to continuous rainfall within the 72 hours. The Weather Service office recorded 8.41 inches of rain for the duration of this period.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface trough slightly moved south of the area as a ridge to the east-northeast migrates toward the Samoan Island through the new week.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The weather service office recorded 5.21 inches of rain for the 48 period. This confirmed several reports of flooded roadways around the island.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A trough lingering along the Samoan Islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Weather Service office in Pago Pago recorded 4.98 inches of rain combined for the 12th and 13th of May.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Wind generated waves associated with an active trough and thunderstorms in the vicinity, have caused seas to reach 10 to 14 feet throughout this period. Two fatalities in the north west and north east shores were reported within a few days apart; both were veteran fishermen.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Surfs of 10 to 14 feet were recorded by the PacIOOS aunu'u buoy during the time of this incident. The fisherman who sadly lost his life went out fishing during low tide in the village of Vailoa, which was the normal routine for him. Recent drownings reported were during low tide in combination with high surf conditions.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Wind generated waves associated with an active trough and thunderstorms in the vicinity, have caused seas to reach 10 to 14 feet throughout this period. Two fatalities in the north west and north east shores were reported within a few days apart; both were veteran fishermen.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Fishermen died due to drowning in Tula (northeastern shore) during low tide conditions. A high surf advisory was also in effect.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An active trough south of the islands associated with tropical storm Sarai and a building ridge north of the trough produced tight pressure gradients over the Samoa Island Chain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The NWS reported wind speeds of 30 MPH with higher gusts that resulted in a power outage noted by the American Samoa Power authority. This even caused conductor clashing, which caused incorrect tensioning of power lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Embedded within the trough was a weak low west of Savaii that moved slowly southwest of the Samoa Island chain. This movement, produced frequent showers, heavy at times and thunderstorms to descend over the islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy precipitation of 3.35 inches of rain over a 24-hour period was recorded at the NWS Pago Pago. As grounds were already saturated prior to this event, many flooded reports in the typical villages with poor drainage systems like Futiga, Tafuna, and Fagaalu were called in. Also, 2 landslides were observed. 1. in Fagaalu 2. in Fatu ma Futi village.
